preview_text:
The model favors Canada heavily, assigning an 85% win probability against a Qatar side given just 0.2% chance of victory. This 85-percentage-point gap reflects an asymmetric matchup in the model's assessment. Qatar's underdog position is stark, though a draw remains possible at 15%.

The model's outcome distribution skews sharply toward a Canadian win, with draws plausible but draws and Qatar wins combined accounting for less than 15% of projected outcomes. The spread suggests Canada enters as a strong favorite; competitive margins are unlikely under this forecast, though the small draw probability signals the match is not predetermined.

Model and sportsbook estimates diverge considerably. The market assigns Canada 62% instead of 85%—a 23-percentage-point difference—and grants Qatar 15% rather than 0.2%. The market also prices draws higher at 23% versus 15%. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct; the gap indicates materially different risk profiles between the two sources.
